picks.rationale: The Knicks are well-rested and playing at home after a dominant 51-point win in their last game. The 76ers are coming off an exhausting seven-game series, and Joel Embiid is playing through a hip contusion, though listed as probable. The spread has moved from an opening of -4.5 to the current -7.5, indicating significant sharp money backing the Knicks. The Knicks have covered this -7.5 line in their last three games and in six of their last ten home games.

Both teams have shown offensive capabilities recently, with the Knicks averaging 113.5 points and the 76ers 106.6 points in their last ten games. The head-to-head average points in their last five matchups combine for 223.2 points, which is above the current total. Although the total line has remained stable, one projection suggests the game could land around 228 points, indicating potential value on the over.

The Minnesota Timberwolves are significantly hampered by injuries, with key players Anthony Edwards and Ayo Dosunmu listed as questionable, and Donte DiVincenzo out for the playoffs. The San Antonio Spurs are well-rested after a 4-1 series win and are playing at home as a top team in the Western Conference. The initial opening spread was even higher at -14.5, suggesting strong market confidence in the Spurs despite a slight adjustment.

The total line for this game has moved up from an opening of 216.5 to 217.5, which often signals sharp money coming in on the Over. Despite the Timberwolves' injuries, their last 10 games have seen them average 118.4 points. Furthermore, four of the last five head-to-head matchups between these two teams have gone Over the total, with several high-scoring affairs.

The New York Knicks are strong favorites at home, boasting a 53-29 season record and a 30-10 home record. They are facing a Philadelphia 76ers team that is fatigued from a grueling seven-game series and dealing with Joel Embiid's hip contusion. The Knicks have won three of the last five head-to-head matchups against the 76ers, including a 49-point victory earlier this season. The betting odds give the Knicks a 75% implied probability of winning this game.

The Carolina Hurricanes are in formidable form, having swept their first-round series and securing a decisive 3-0 victory in Game 1 against the Flyers. Their home advantage is significant, with the Hurricanes boasting nine consecutive home wins against Metropolitan Division opponents. Furthermore, goaltender Frederik Andersen has been exceptional in the postseason, maintaining a .961 save percentage and a 0.90 goals-against average. The Flyers' 5-on-5 defense has shown vulnerabilities, ranking in the bottom three with a 12.89 xGA, which Carolina's potent offense is well-positioned to exploit.

picks.pick: Logan Stankoven Over 2.5 Shots on Goal
picks.rationale: Logan Stankoven has been a consistent offensive threat for the Hurricanes, scoring in five consecutive playoff games and playing a pivotal role on Carolina's most dangerous forward line. His line contributed all three goals in Game 1, highlighting his active involvement in offensive generation. Stankoven is averaging over 0.30 Expected Goals per game, indicating his propensity for high-quality scoring chances. Given his hot streak and central role in the Hurricanes' attack, he is highly likely to exceed 2.5 shots on goal in this contest.

The Anaheim Ducks have demonstrated a surprising edge over the Golden Knights this season, winning all three of their regular-season matchups. A significant situational factor is Vegas playing on a back-to-back after a demanding Game 6 victory yesterday, which could lead to fatigue against Anaheim's high-powered offense. While the Golden Knights possess playoff experience, the Ducks' ability to generate offense through speed and transition, coupled with Vegas's potential weariness, makes the +1.5 spread a strong play.

Both the Ducks and Golden Knights have exhibited goaltending struggles in the playoffs, with Lukas Dostal (ANA) averaging 3.87 Goals Against per game and Carter Hart (VGK) posting a 2.72 GAA and .898 save percentage. The Ducks' offense has been high-powered, leading the first round in scoring, and the Golden Knights' lineup features potent offensive threats like Jack Eichel and Mitch Marner. Considering Vegas is on a back-to-back and both teams' defensive vulnerabilities, this game is poised to be a high-scoring affair, pushing the total over 6.5 goals.

The Carolina Hurricanes are a dominant force, entering this Game 2 with a perfect 5-0-0 record in their last five games and a 100% win probability. They swept their first-round opponent and convincingly won Game 1 against the Flyers 3-0. Carolina's goaltender, Frederik Andersen, has been stellar, leading all playoff backstops with a .961 save percentage and a 0.90 goals-against average. With a strong home record against Metropolitan Division opponents and a healthy, high-performing lineup, the Hurricanes are a high-confidence pick to win outright.

The New York Yankees, with a strong 23-11 record, are facing a struggling Baltimore Orioles team that is 15-19 and on a three-game losing streak. Yankees starting pitcher Cam Schlittler has been identified as one of the best pitchers in Major League Baseball this season. Despite some key injuries on both sides, the Yankees' ace on the mound at home against a slumping opponent provides a significant advantage. Public betting heavily favors the Yankees, with 91% of bets on them.

Both the Cincinnati Reds and Chicago Cubs have demonstrated a strong trend towards high-scoring games recently, with each team hitting the Over in 8 of their last 10 outings. Both squads are also dealing with significant pitching injuries, including key starters like Justin Steele and Hunter Greene on the injured list. This weakened pitching environment, combined with the recent offensive trends, strongly supports a high-scoring affair. The elevated total of 11.5 reflects expectations for a slugfest, making the Over a confident play.

picks.game: Chicago White Sox @ Los Angeles Angels
picks.rationale: The Chicago White Sox are in significantly better recent form, boasting a 7-3 record in their last 10 games, a stark contrast to the Los Angeles Angels' dismal 2-8 stretch. The Angels are also experiencing a pitching change, with George Klassen stepping in to start due to Ryan Johnson's illness, which can disrupt team stability. Despite being on the road, the White Sox's current momentum and the Angels' struggles, compounded by their key injuries, make the White Sox at plus money a valuable pick.

The Atlanta Braves hold the best record in MLB at 25-10 and are coming off a series sweep, while the Seattle Mariners were just swept in their last series. Although the Braves are without superstar Ronald Acuña Jr. due to a hamstring strain, their overall team strength and superior recent performance are undeniable. Getting the league's top team at plus money against a struggling opponent, even with a rookie pitcher in JR Ritchie who has performed well in his limited starts, presents a strong value bet.

This game is scheduled to be played at Coors Field, which is widely recognized as a high-scoring environment due to its altitude. The Colorado Rockies' starting pitcher is TBD, and they recently surrendered 11 runs in their previous game. Given the significant park factors favoring offense and the Rockies' recent struggles on the mound, a high-scoring affair is highly probable. The Over 10.5 is a confident selection in this hitter-friendly venue.

Arsenal's attack is significantly hampered by the likely absence of key players Martin Odegaard and Kai Havertz, who were missing from training on May 4, 2026, and are expected to be unavailable for this crucial second leg. Atlético Madrid, under Diego Simeone, is renowned for its disciplined defensive approach, which consistently leads to low-scoring matches. Four of the last five competitive head-to-head encounters between these teams have finished with Under 2.5 goals, including the 1-1 draw in the first leg of this semi-final. This combination of Arsenal's offensive struggles due to injuries and Atlético's defensive prowess strongly supports a low-scoring outcome in this high-stakes match.

Arsenal faces a significant challenge with key attacking midfielders Martin Odegaard and Kai Havertz likely sidelined, severely impacting their creative and goal-scoring threat. Conversely, Atlético Madrid receives a major boost with the return of several key players, including Julian Alvarez, Alexander Sorloth, and Jose Gimenez, all named in the squad and expected to be available after resting in their last league game. With the aggregate score tied at 1-1, Atlético's proven ability to grind out results in tight Champions League knockout matches and their disciplined defensive structure make them a dangerous underdog, offering substantial value at +425 for a potential upset or a draw that would see them advance.

Arsenal holds a formidable home advantage, boasting an excellent home record of 14 wins, 2 draws, and only 2 losses in the Premier League this season. They have also maintained an unbeaten record in the Champions League campaign, showcasing their consistency and strength in European competition. Historically, Arsenal demonstrated their capability to dominate Atlético Madrid at home with a commanding 4-0 victory in October 2025. Despite some key injuries, their overall squad depth, home support, and strong motivation to advance in this tied semi-final provide a solid foundation for a victory.

Despite the first leg being a low-scoring affair, Arsenal possesses significant offensive potential at home, as demonstrated by their previous 4-0 thrashing of Atlético Madrid. Atlético Madrid has shown vulnerability in away Champions League matches this season, notably conceding multiple goals in 3-2 losses to Liverpool and Tottenham. With the aggregate score tied at 1-1, both teams will eventually need to push for goals to secure progression, which could lead to a more open game with increased scoring opportunities. The dynamics of a crucial second leg often result in more offensive play as time winds down, favoring the Over.
